commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh He trusted in the LORD God of Israel; so that after him was none like him among all the kings of Judah, nor any that were before him. Ezechias was remarkable for many excellent qualities. Yet we must not push these comparisons too far, contrary to the intention of the sacred writers. The same eulogium is given to Josias, (chap. ) and David seems to be preferred, chap. These three are particularly commended, Ecclesiasticus xlix. (Calmet) Their virtues were certainly different in some respects.
